violations.text: Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as pulled pork) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

Chicken wings, cheese, and buffalo chicken dip was observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit in the reach-in cooler inside the kitchen. Foods moved to walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

Tuna was observed held at 49 degrees Fahrenheit in the reach-in cooler. Per PIC, food has been in the cooler overnight. PIC voluntarily disposed of food at time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of biofilm. The ice machine must be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent the contamination of ice. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

The floors behind the refrigeration units and behind the shelving area in the basement has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026
